_comment: Under CoALA, each MCP server is an additional GROUNDING action surface
(§4.2). Adding a server extends the external action space; removing one
shrinks it. Treat additions as architectural changes, not config tweaks.
Secrets come from Railway environment variables — never hard-code tokens
here. The ${VAR} syntax is expanded at load time.
Relationship to peers and channels (see AGENTS.md §6 — dormant by default):
- Peers (if any) are modeled in PEERS.md on the volume.
- Channels are configured via config.yaml (messaging gateways, e.g.
telegram.allowed_chats) and the principal's memory-key contract
To add a server, copy the `github` entry below. Hermes ignores any key
slimness — recover them from git history if needed.)
